content: Current LLM self-reflection mechanisms that leverage inconsistent multiple perspectives can be framed as a multi-start parallel extension of the original single-path Self-Refine framework, turning sequential iterative refinement into a self-bootstrapping search for higher-quality outputs that still preserves Self-Refine's core advantage of no additional training or supervision. This approach addresses a key limitation of vanilla Self-Refine: it can get stuck in local suboptimal output optima by only refining along one single initial output path.
